description: Tempdoc 569 §16 #1 — the declaration-default single-authority register (the gate keeping the inversion inverted). 569's central claim is 'the prevention is the register/gate whose coverage projects from the authority's catalog.' §14/§15 made four surfaces (Settings, Library, Help, Health) declaration-default under ONE built-in declaration (CORE_DECLARED, themes/builtinPresentations.ts): each surface renders its region(s) through the DeclaredSurface engine (activeBodyFor(regionId) -> <jf-declared-surface>) instead of hand-painted Lit. This register is the CATALOG (which regions are declaration-default + which x-ui-renderer hints their bodies reference); the gate (check-declared-surfaces.mjs) enforces POSITIVE COVERAGE — every `regions` entry's surface must actually mount the engine for its region, and every `renderers` hint must resolve to exactly one registered renderer. It deliberately does NOT scan for 'is a second hand-rolled authority co-existing alongside the mount' — a degrade-never-fail fallback render is syntactically indistinguishable from a forbidden fork (the same lesson as governance/composition-surfaces.v1.json §13.3). The anti-drift teeth are POSITIVE: a regression that reverts a surface to pure hand-Lit drops the activeBodyFor/<jf-declared-surface> mount and FAILS the gate. Honest ceiling (565 §12.10): an import-invisible re-model slips — early-warning register, the accepted norm for every presentation gate. A NEW declaration-default region/renderer is a discovery step (add the row + route it through the engine), review-gated, not scan-gated. Lighter scripts/ci tier; wired as a ci.yml step + the CLAUDE.md pre-merge list.

engine.rationale: The ONE presentation engine — DeclaredSurface (components/DeclaredSurface.ts, tag <jf-declared-surface>, display:contents) renders a SurfaceBodyDeclaration resolved by activeBodyFor(regionId) from the active presentation runtime (state/presentationRuntime.ts). A surface is declaration-default iff it imports activeBodyFor and mounts <jf-declared-surface> for its region(s).
